results.title: Top 10 LLM observability tools: Complete guide for 2025 - Braintrust
results.content: LLM observability goes far beyond basic logging, requiring real-time monitoring of prompts and responses, tracking token usage, measuring latency, attributing costs, and evaluating the effectiveness of individual prompts across your entire AI stack. At Braintrust, we offer the leading LLM observability platform combining integrations with all major LLMs and AI frameworks, paired with intuitive interfaces that let everyone on your team understand how AI features are functioning. Each integration serves production AI applications with automatic cost tracking, latency monitoring, evaluation metrics, production alerting, and tool tracing that work immediately. **Unified ML/AI observability**: MLflow monitors both classical ML models and modern LLMs from a single platform, simplifying toolchain management for teams working across both paradigms. Galileo provides enterprise-focused AI observability with support for major LLM providers and comprehensive agent framework integration, serving organizations with strict security and compliance requirements. The right observability platform provides the essential visibility that lets teams deploy AI systems confidently, knowing they can understand and control behavior even as complexity scales.

results.title: Best LLM Observability Tools in 2026 - Firecrawl
results.content: Langfuse LLM observability platform dashboard showing traces, prompt management, and evaluation metrics. Langfuse LLM observability platform dashboard showing traces, prompt management, and evaluation metrics. The platform covers the full observability stack: tracing with multi-turn conversation support, prompt versioning with a built-in playground, and flexible evaluation through LLM-as-judge, user feedback, or custom metrics. Opik LLM observability tool by Comet showing tracing and evaluation capabilities. Opik LLM observability tool by Comet showing tracing and evaluation capabilities. Confident AI LLM observability platform showing evaluation-first tracing and quality monitoring dashboard. Confident AI LLM observability platform showing evaluation-first tracing and quality monitoring dashboard. Confident AI is an evaluation-first LLM observability platform built around DeepEval, one of the most widely adopted open-source LLM evaluation frameworks. Arize Phoenix open source LLM evaluation tool with OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics. Arize Phoenix open source LLM evaluation tool with OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics. A common setup is a gateway tool (Helicone or Portkey) for cost tracking and routing, paired with an evaluation tool (Phoenix or TruLens) for quality metrics.

results.title: 7 best free and open source LLM observability tools - PostHog
results.content: PostHog is an all-in-one developer platform that combines LLM observability with several other developer-focused tools, such as product and web analytics, session replay, feature flags, experiments, error tracking, and surveys. Beyond integrations with popular LLM providers, tracing, and an aggregate analytics dashboard, Helicone provides more tools like prompt management and evals. * Want LLM observability running alongside product analytics, session replay, A/B testing, feature flags, and more in one platform for full visibility? * You want LLM observability connected to the rest of your product data – session replays, feature flags, A/B testing, and analytics all in one place. It typically includes capturing individual LLM calls (inputs, outputs, latency, token usage), aggregating metrics across requests, and providing tools to debug issues and improve model performance. A good LLM observability tool gives you visibility into how your AI-powered app is performing in production. Do I need a separate LLM observability tool if I already use PostHog?

results.title: LLM Observability: How to Monitor and Optimize LLMs - WitnessAI
results.content: ## What is LLM Observability? LLM observability refers to the practice of collecting, analyzing, and visualizing key metrics and signals from large language models (LLMs) to ensure their performance, accuracy, and reliability across various AI applications. Much like traditional application observability, LLM observability provides visibility into the internal workings of LLM apps, enabling teams to debug issues, monitor model behavior, and optimize the user experience. LLM observability involves instrumenting LLM applications and integrating observability tools to track real-time data, events, and metrics throughout the LLM lifecycle. 5. **Debugging & Alerting**: Observability tools support root cause analysis, issue detection, and automated alerts for LLM anomalies. Observability solutions help enforce data protection policies across LLM applications. LLM observability provides behavioral analytics to detect anomalous usage patterns, such as excessive querying, prompt engineering attempts, or misuse of generative AI for data exfiltration. ## LLM Observability for Your Models. LLM observability is essential for building secure, high-performing, and cost-effective generative AI applications.

results.title: LLM Observability & Application Tracing (Open Source) - Langfuse
results.content: # Observability & Application Tracing. The core of this is **application tracing** — structured logs of every request that capture the exact prompt sent, the model's response, token usage, latency, and any tools or retrieval steps in between. Take a moment to understand the core concepts of tracing in Langfuse: traces, sessions, and observations. What is the difference between observability and tracing? **Tracing** is a specific observability technique that records the flow of a request through your system, preserving causal relationships between operations. In the context of LLM applications, tracing is the most important observability tool because it captures the full context of each request — prompts, responses, tool calls, and their relationships. Each trace captures every operation — LLM calls, retrieval steps, tool executions, and custom logic — along with timing, inputs, outputs, and metadata. Trace events are queued locally and flushed in batches, so your application's response time is not affected.
